message:
feat(cli): add extension flag passthrough and fix print-mode empty input handling

**CLI extension flags** (cli.rs): Implement two-pass CLI parsing that
separates known pi flags from unknown extension-specific flags. The new
`parse_with_extension_flags()` first attempts standard clap parsing; on
failure it runs `preprocess_extension_flags()` which identifies known
long options (--model, --provider, --extension, etc.) and short flags
(-v, -c, -r, -p, -e), passes them through to clap, and extracts
everything else as ExtensionCliFlag values with optional string payloads.
This allows extensions to register custom --flags without modifying the
core CLI parser.

Includes tests for: extension flag extraction in second-pass, boolean
flags without values, and validation that subcommand parsing still works.

**Print mode** (main.rs): Fix validation ordering so that `pi -p --mode
json` with no input message emits a valid empty JSON session header
instead of bailing with "No input provided". The error-on-empty check
now runs after the session header is printed in JSON mode, and the
"No messages were sent" error is similarly gated to text mode only.
Error/Aborted stop-reason handling is moved inside the text-mode branch
since JSON consumers handle stop reasons in their own parsing logic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
